evals.text: Output contains exactly these five subsections in this order: What is working, Issues, Recommended changes, Failure modes, Minimal rewrite plan. Any consistent heading level (H2 or H3) is acceptable as long as it matches the skill's Output-format spec.
evals.pass: All five required subsections present, in exact order, under a consistent heading level. No extra sibling subsections at the same level.
evals.fail: Any required subsection missing, out of order, or an unexpected sibling subsection included.

evals.text: The Issues subsection contains at most five items.

Every item in Recommended changes begins with an imperative verb (add, remove, require, rename, replace, extract, guard, wrap, surface, split, tighten, cap, etc.). No questions. No gerunds or noun phrases as the lead word.
evals.pass: All Recommended-changes items start with an imperative verb.
evals.fail: One or more items phrased as a question, suggestion, gerund, or noun phrase.

evals.text: Is this review more actionable than the baseline review for the same input?
evals.rubric: A review is 'more actionable' when a reader could act on at least 80% of its Recommended-changes items without asking clarifying questions. Compare only the Recommended-changes subsection, not the other sections.

inputs.description: A 3-line vague prompt ('You are an expert. Review my draft and tell me what's wrong. Be thorough.'). Represents the 'tiny input' case where the reviewer must still produce a well-structured critique.
inputs.prompt: Apply craft-reflect to the prompt in this file.

inputs.description: Snapshot of skills/craft-blueprint/SKILL.md at baseline time (96 lines). A real CraftKit skill with moderate surface area.
inputs.prompt: Apply craft-reflect to the skill in this file.

inputs.description: Snapshot of skills/craft-prompt/SKILL.md at baseline time (137 lines). A longer skill absorbed from prompt-builder with multiple sections and tables.
inputs.prompt: Apply craft-reflect to the skill in this file.

notes: Baseline E1 check: the current craft-reflect SKILL.md uses H3 for Output-format subsections. E1 accepts any consistent heading level that matches the skill's own spec.
